{"key_event":"Union militancy and post-imperial economic constraint","reasoning":"Civil society was already extremely powerful, and high union leverage, cultural liberalization, civil-liberties reforms, protest politics, and the Ombudsman slightly increased its capacity to constrain authority. The state’s domestic administrative reach remained overwhelming through welfare, planning, wage policy, and immigration controls, but decolonization, sterling crises, and dependence on organized interests modestly reduced its autonomy from the late-1950s level.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":9.9,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":9.4}
